engineGuide.h2: Honda N-WGN Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Honda N-WGN across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 0.66L Petrol
entries.history: First-gen 660cc 3-cylinder DOHC Earth Dreams engine for JDM N-WGN imports, known for efficiency but PCV-related oil consumption.
compatibleVariants: G, L, Custom, Custom Turbo (JH1/JH2 chassis, FWD/4WD)
commonFailures: PCV system/oil separator failure causing oil consumption (OEM 11310-RZC-W02)
Timing chain tensioner wear leading to rattle at 80k+ km
Turbo wastegate actuator failure on turbo variants (OEM 17240-RZC-A01)
entries.cta: Get quotes for S07A Honda N-WGN engine replacement

entries.title: 0.66L Petrol
entries.history: Updated second-generation S07 with revised cooling and direct injection, addressing some S07A weaknesses but introducing carbon buildup concerns.
compatibleVariants: G, L, Custom, Custom Turbo (JH3/JH4 chassis, FWD/4WD)
commonFailures: Carbon buildup on intake valves due to direct injection (requires walnut blasting)
Timing chain stretch on high-mileage units
Turbo wastegate actuator wear on turbo variants
entries.cta: Get quotes for S07B Honda N-WGN engine replacement

entries.title: 0.66L Petrol Turbo

commonFailures: Turbo wastegate actuator failure (OEM 17240-RZC-A01) causing boost loss
PCV system oil consumption exacerbated by turbo pressure
Intercooler piping leaks on high-mileage units

commonFailures: Wastegate actuator wear on high-mileage turbo units
Carbon buildup on intake valves (direct injection)
Turbo oil seal leaks under sustained load

commonProblems.h2: Honda N-WGN Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Honda N-WGN's S07A and S07B 660cc engines are generally reliable but suffer from well-documented oil consumption and turbo wastegate issues. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded PCV components and timing chain parts designed to address these original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Oil Consumption - Why S07A/S07B Engines Burn Oil
problems.affectedModels: Honda N-WGN G, L, Custom, Custom Turbo (2013-2025, S07A & S07B)
problems.rootCause: PCV system oil separator (OEM 11310-RZC-W02) fails, allowing oil vapour into intake manifold and combustion chambers.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace oil separator (11311-RZC-W02), PCV valve, clean intake
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative fix; only viable before severe oil burning begins

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace piston rings, valve seals, upgraded PCV, clean carbon
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses moderate oil consumption; suitable if block is sound

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt unit with upgraded PCV, timing components, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage engines; includes 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: If your N-WGN is burning more than 1L per 1,000km, a full rebuilt engine with upgraded PCV system offers better long-term value than repeated top-end repairs. For early symptoms, the PCV upgrade alone may suffice.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt S07A/S07B engine prices for your Honda N-WGN

problems.h4: Turbo Wastegate Failure - Boost Loss on Turbo Models
problems.affectedModels: Honda N-WGN Custom Turbo (2015-2025, S07A-T & S07B-T)
problems.rootCause: Wastegate actuator (OEM 17240-RZC-A01) diaphragm fatigues, causing boost pressure loss and reduced performance.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace wastegate actuator only, recalibrate boost
repairOptions.longevity: Quick fix; may recur if turbo internals are worn

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuild turbo with new bearings, seals, upgraded actuator
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses internal wear; suitable if engine block is healthy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo assembly
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage turbo engines; includes warranty

problems.recommendation: For Custom Turbo models over 80,000km showing boost loss, a rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo assembly prevents repeat failures and offers warranty protection.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Honda N-WGN Custom Turbo engine replacement

problems.h4: Timing Chain Stretch - Rattle and Potential Engine Damage
problems.affectedModels: Honda N-WGN all variants (2013-2025, S07A & S07B)
problems.rootCause: Chain tensioner wear allows chain to stretch, causing timing rattle on cold start and potential valve interference if skipped.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides; reset timing
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative; only viable before chain skips or damages valves

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt unit with upgraded timing components
repairOptions.longevity: Required if chain has skipped or caused valve damage

problems.recommendation: Listen for cold-start rattle. If present, replace chain/tensioner immediately. If the engine has already suffered valve contact, a rebuilt engine is the only reliable solution.
problems.cta: Compare Honda N-WGN timing chain repair vs replacement costs

problems.h4: Carbon Buildup on Valves - Hard Starting & Misfires (S07B)
problems.affectedModels: Honda N-WGN JH3/JH4 variants (2019-2025, S07B direct injection)
problems.rootCause: Direct injection design allows carbon deposits to accumulate on intake valves, reducing airflow and causing misfires.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove intake manifold, walnut-shell blast valves clean
Clean valves, replace seals, inspect guides, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if misfires persist after cleaning

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt unit with cleaned/prepped cylinder head
repairOptions.longevity: Best for engines with severe carbon damage or high mileage

problems.recommendation: For S07B engines showing hard starting or misfires, start with walnut blasting. If symptoms persist or mileage exceeds 100,000km, a rebuilt engine offers better long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Enquire about Honda N-WGN S07B carbon buildup solutions

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Honda N-WGN
finalCta.paragraph: The Honda N-WGN's compact 660cc engines are economical but prone to specific, well-documented failures. EngineMarket connects you with UK JDM import specialists who supply rebuilt engines with upgraded PCV systems, timing components, and turbo assemblies-addressing the root causes of oil consumption, chain stretch, and wastegate failure. Compare prices from vetted suppliers, all backed by a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Honda N-WGN Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Honda N-WGN and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.title: Honda N-WGN Petrol Engines
items.description: All Honda N-WGN models sold in the UK are petrol-only Kei cars, powered exclusively by the 658cc S07A (first-gen) or S07B (second-gen) three-cylinder Earth Dreams engine family.
items.descriptor: All Honda N-WGN models sold in the UK are petrol-only Kei cars, powered exclusively by the 658cc S07A (first-gen) or S07B (second-gen) three-cylinder Earth Dreams engine family.
items.familiesLabel: Common Honda N-WGN Petrol Engine Families
families: S07A - First-gen 658cc NA/turbo (JH1/JH2, 2013-2019), known for PCV-related oil consumption
S07B - Second-gen 658cc NA/turbo (JH3/JH4, 2019+), improved cooling but prone to intake valve carbon buildup
S07A-T/S07B-T - Turbocharged variants (64hp), popular but susceptible to wastegate actuator failure (OEM 17240-RZC-A01)
foundIn: First generation (JH1/JH2 chassis) (2013-2019) - S07A NA (58hp) / S07A-T turbo (64hp)
Second generation (JH3/JH4 chassis) (2019-2025) - S07B NA (58hp) / S07B-T turbo (64hp)
knownFor: Excellent fuel economy (25-30 km/L) and low road tax as Kei-compliant imports
PCV system oil consumption on S07A engines (upgraded separator 11311-RZC-W02 fixes this)
Timing chain stretch on high-mileage units; listen for cold-start rattle
Turbo wastegate actuator failure on Custom Turbo models causing boost loss
typicalModels: Honda N-WGN Custom Turbo (performance trim, turbo)

engineYears.h2: Honda N-WGN Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Honda N-WGN's production history, organised so UK import owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from JDM specialists.

years.description: The Honda N-WGN launched in Japan with the new S07A Earth Dreams engine, establishing the 658cc three-cylinder platform for all future variants.
keyChanges: Launch of first-generation N-WGN (JH1 chassis) with S07A naturally aspirated engine (58hp)
Introduction of Kei-compliant 658cc displacement, DOHC 12-valve design
CVT automatic transmission standard across all variants
mainEngines: S07A - 0.66L petrol NA (G, L trims, FWD)
knownFor: Strong fuel economy and low running costs as a Kei import
Early PCV system design prone to oil consumption at higher mileage
Simple, accessible engine bay facilitating DIY maintenance
years.cta: Get quotes for 2013 Honda N-WGN replacement engines
years.ticker: Honda N-WGN 2013 engine replacement • S07A oil consumption fix • N-WGN PCV upgrade

years.description: The JH2 facelift introduced the turbocharged S07A-T variant and minor PCV revisions, expanding the N-WGN's appeal to performance-minded Kei buyers.
keyChanges: Introduction of Custom Turbo trim with S07A-T turbo engine (64hp)
Minor revisions to PCV system and ECU mapping for improved emissions
Optional 4WD system available on select variants
mainEngines: S07A - 0.66L petrol NA (G, L, Custom, FWD/4WD)
knownFor: Turbo variant delivers lively performance while retaining Kei tax benefits
Wastegate actuator (17240-RZC-A01) emerges as common turbo failure point
PCV oil consumption persists on high-mileage NA units
years.ticker: N-WGN turbo wastegate repair • S07A PCV upgrade cost • JH2 4WD engine compatibility

years.description: The all-new JH3 chassis debuted with the updated S07B engine, featuring revised cooling passages and direct injection for improved efficiency.
keyChanges: Launch of second-generation N-WGN (JH3 chassis) with S07B engine family
Introduction of direct injection on S07B (increasing carbon buildup risk)
Updated ECU mapping and emissions compliance for newer regulations
mainEngines: S07B - 0.66L petrol NA (G, L, Custom, FWD/4WD)
knownFor: S07B offers marginal efficiency gains over S07A but introduces direct injection complexity
Intake valve carbon buildup becomes a documented issue requiring walnut blasting
Turbo variants retain wastegate actuator vulnerability despite minor design tweaks

years.ticker: S07B carbon buildup cleaning • JH3 engine compatibility check • N-WGN 2019 replacement cost

S07B engine receives updated ECU calibration for stricter emissions standards
Minor component revisions to water pump and tensioner designs

faq.h2: Honda N-WGN Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Honda N-WGN engine in the UK?
items.answer: Replacing a Honda N-WGN engine typically costs between £900 and £1,600 for a rebuilt unit, depending on whether you need the naturally aspirated S07A/S07B or the turbocharged variant. Used engines start from around £600 but carry higher risk due to known oil consumption issues.
keyPoints: Rebuilt S07A naturally aspirated: £900-£1,300 supply only
Rebuilt S07A/S07B turbo: £1,100-£1,600 supply only
Used engines from £600 but verify PCV history and mileage
Supply & fit labour typically adds £300-£600 depending on region
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Honda N-WGN engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: Why is my Honda N-WGN burning so much oil?
items.answer: Excessive oil consumption in the Honda N-WGN is most commonly caused by PCV system failure, specifically the oil separator (OEM 11310-RZC-W02), which allows oil vapour into the intake and combustion chambers.
keyPoints: PCV oil separator failure is the #1 cause of N-WGN oil burning
Upgraded separator part 11311-RZC-W02 addresses the design weakness
Symptoms include blue exhaust smoke, frequent top-ups, fouled spark plugs
Ignoring it can lead to carbon buildup, misfires and catalytic converter damage
items.cta: Get quotes for Honda N-WGN PCV-upgraded rebuilt engines

items.question: Does the Honda N-WGN have a timing belt or chain?
items.answer: All Honda N-WGN engines (S07A and S07B) use a timing chain, not a belt. While chains are generally more durable, they can stretch on high-mileage units, causing rattle on cold start and potential timing issues.
keyPoints: S07A/S07B engines use a maintenance-free timing chain design
Chain tensioner wear can cause rattle at 90,000+ km
If the chain skips, valve damage may occur requiring full rebuild
Replacement chain kits cost £500-£850 specialist price
items.cta: Compare Honda N-WGN timing chain repair vs replacement costs

items.question: What's the difference between S07A and S07B engines?
items.answer: The S07A powers first-generation N-WGN (JH1/JH2, 2013-2019), while the S07B is the updated unit for second-gen models (JH3/JH4, 2019+). The S07B features revised cooling and direct injection but is not directly interchangeable with S07A.
keyPoints: S07A: First-gen, port injection, known for PCV oil consumption
S07B: Second-gen, direct injection, prone to intake valve carbon buildup
Engines are not interchangeable without ECU, harness and mount modifications
Both share the same 658cc displacement and 58/64hp output ratings
items.cta: Enquire about Honda N-WGN S07A vs S07B compatibility

items.question: Are Honda N-WGN engines reliable?
items.answer: Honda N-WGN engines are generally reliable when maintained properly, but specific known issues-PCV-related oil consumption, timing chain stretch, and turbo wastegate failure-require proactive attention to avoid costly repairs.
keyPoints: Regular oil changes with 0W-20 synthetic help prevent PCV clogging
Listen for cold-start chain rattle indicating tensioner wear
Turbo models need wastegate actuator inspection at 80,000+ km
Rebuilt engines with upgraded components offer best long-term reliability
items.cta: Find trusted Honda N-WGN engine specialists with warranty coverage

items.question: Is it worth rebuilding an N-WGN engine or should I replace it?
items.answer: For most Honda N-WGN owners, a rebuilt engine offers better value than a used unit or major repair, as it addresses known failure points with upgraded components and includes a 12-month warranty.
keyPoints: Rebuilt cost (£900-£1,600) often comparable to used + immediate repairs
Upgraded PCV, timing parts and turbo components prevent repeat failures
Warranty protection reduces financial risk versus private import purchases
Ideal if the car is otherwise in good condition with low chassis mileage
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt Honda N-WGN engines with 12-month warranty

items.question: Where can I buy a Honda N-WGN engine in the UK?
items.answer: Honda N-WGN engines are available through UK specialists in Japanese Domestic Market imports. Comparing quotes from multiple vetted suppliers ensures you get the best price and warranty terms for your specific chassis code.
keyPoints: All UK N-WGN engines are JDM imports; verify Japanese mileage documentation
Choose suppliers offering upgraded PCV components for S07A units
Confirm compatibility with your chassis code (JH1/2 vs JH3/4) before ordering
EngineMarket's comparison tool connects you with 5+ specialists instantly
items.cta: Compare Honda N-WGN engine prices from UK JDM import specialists

items.question: Can I fit a turbo engine into my non-turbo Honda N-WGN?
items.answer: Technically yes, but converting a naturally aspirated N-WGN to turbo requires extensive modifications including ECU, wiring harness, intercooler, exhaust and fuel system changes-often costing more than buying a factory turbo model.
keyPoints: Turbo swap requires matching ECU, harness, intercooler and exhaust components
Fuel pump and injectors may need upgrading for turbo boost pressures
Legal compliance (IVA/MOT) can be complex for modified Kei imports
Selling your NA model and buying a factory Custom Turbo is usually more economical
items.cta: Enquire about Honda N-WGN Custom Turbo engine availability

items.question: What oil should I use in my Honda N-WGN engine?
items.answer: Honda recommends 0W-20 fully synthetic oil for all S07A and S07B engines. Using the correct viscosity and changing it every 5,000km helps prevent PCV clogging and timing chain wear.
keyPoints: 0W-20 fully synthetic meets Honda Earth Dreams specifications
Change interval: every 5,000km or 6 months for optimal protection
Using thicker oil can worsen PCV-related oil consumption issues
Always replace oil filter with genuine or OEM-equivalent part
items.cta: Find Honda N-WGN engine suppliers offering pre-filled oil service

items.question: How do I identify which engine is in my Honda N-WGN?
items.answer: Check your chassis code (JH1/JH2 = S07A, 2013-2019; JH3/JH4 = S07B, 2019+) or locate the engine code stamped on the block near the bellhousing. Your VIN plate also confirms the model variant.
keyPoints: Chassis code found on VIN plate under bonnet or driver's door jamb
Engine code stamped on block: S07A, S07B, S07A-T or S07B-T
Turbo variants have intercooler piping and turbocharger visible on top
Enter your registration on EngineMarket for instant engine identification
items.cta: Get instant Honda N-WGN engine identification via registration lookup
